```markdown
---
applyTo: '**'
---
# BambiSleep Chat Code Generation Guidelines

## Core Principles

- **Simplicity First**: Write minimal, straightforward code; one function = one task
- **Follow Existing Patterns**: Observe and reuse patterns already in the codebase
- **Minimal Documentation**: Brief comments only for non-obvious logic
- **Direct Solutions**: Favor direct code over abstractions or "best practices"

## Technical Implementation

### JavaScript
- Use plain JS over frameworks
- Structure as IIFE modules with clear public API
- Use event dispatchers for cross-module communication
- Always check elements exist before manipulating them
- Always verify socket connections before emitting

### EJS Templates
- Keep logic in templates minimal
- Use includes for reusable components
- Follow existing file naming conventions

```ejs
<%- include('partials/header') %>
<div class="main-content">
  <% if (data.items && data.items.length > 0) { %>
    <% data.items.forEach(item => { %>
      <div class="item"><%= item.name %></div>
    <% }); %>
  <% } %>
</div>
```

### Socket.io
- Follow naming pattern: `client-action-verb` and `server-action-verb`
- Keep payloads small and focused
- Update UI directly rather than refreshing entire page

```javascript
// Listen for updates
socket.on('server-update-status', data => {
  const statusEl = document.getElementById('status');
  if (statusEl) statusEl.textContent = data.status;
});

// Send updates
function sendUpdate(data) {
  if (window.socket && window.socket.connected) {
    window.socket.emit('client-update-request', data);
  }
}
```

### DOM Updates
- Batch DOM operations with DocumentFragment
- Store element references for cleanup
- Debounce event handlers for performance

```javascript
const fragment = document.createDocumentFragment();
items.forEach(item => {
  const el = document.createElement('div');
  el.textContent = item.name;
  fragment.appendChild(el);
});
container.appendChild(fragment);
```

### State Management
- Use centralized state (bambiSystem) when available
- Persist with localStorage when appropriate
- Use events to communicate state changes

## Avoid
- Over-engineering solutions
- Extensive error handling for unlikely scenarios
- Deep nesting of functions or callbacks
- Adding dependencies unless absolutely necessary
